default.description: A new process was created. Sysmon Event ID 1 captures process creation with full command line. Review image name and command line for signs of abuse.
default.recommendation: Review parent process, command line, and spawning user. Investigate if the process is unexpected for this host.

process_rules.description: PowerShell was executed. Encoded or hidden commands (-enc, -nop, -w hidden) are strong indicators of malicious scripting activity.
process_rules.recommendation: Decode the base64 command. Review PowerShell logs (Event 4103/4104). Block if policy violation; escalate to Tier-2 if encoded.

process_rules.description: cmd.exe was executed with command arguments. Attackers use cmd.exe to run reconnaissance or lateral movement commands.
process_rules.recommendation: Review the full command line. Correlate with other events on the same host. Block suspicious commands.

process_rules.description: reg.exe was used to modify the registry. Persistence is commonly established by adding entries to Run keys in HKCU or HKLM.
process_rules.recommendation: Examine the registry key and value being modified. Remove unauthorized persistence entries. Scan the executable referenced in the value.

process_rules.description: explorer.exe was launched. This is the Windows shell and is typically benign when spawned by a normal user session.
process_rules.recommendation: Verify this was spawned by a user action. Investigate only if parent process is unusual (e.g., spawned by cmd.exe or PowerShell).

default.description: A network connection was initiated. Sysmon Event ID 3 captures outbound connections including destination IP and initiating process.
default.recommendation: Verify the destination IP reputation. Investigate if connection is initiated by an unexpected process. Check for external IPs in threat intel.

process_rules.description: PowerShell established a network connection. This is a strong indicator of a C2 callback. Attackers often use PowerShell to download payloads or beacon to C2 servers.
process_rules.recommendation: Block the destination IP immediately. Isolate the host. Acquire memory and disk forensic image. Escalate to Tier-2 incident response.

default.description: A successful logon event was recorded. Review source IP and account type. After failed logon activity, a successful logon may indicate account compromise.
default.recommendation: Correlate with prior failed logon events. If preceded by brute force activity, treat as possible account compromise and reset credentials.

default.description: A failed logon attempt was recorded. Isolated failures may be benign (mistyped password). Multiple failures from the same IP suggest brute force or credential stuffing.
default.recommendation: Monitor for repeated failures. Enable account lockout policy. Block the source IP if failures exceed threshold.

default.description: A driver or DLL was loaded into a process. Sysmon Event ID 7 captures image loads. Suspicious or unsigned DLLs indicate DLL injection or hijacking.
default.recommendation: Check the DLL signature and path. Investigate if loaded by an unexpected process. Quarantine and analyze the DLL. Check VirusTotal hash.

default.description: This Event ID is not yet mapped in mitre_mapping.json.
default.recommendation: Add this Event ID to mitre_mapping.json to enable automatic classification.
